prompt:
Use 10 sentences to describe the the pattern of the risotto:

    Your ouput should be in the following format:
    <sentence>sentence 1</sentence>
    <sentence>sentence 2</sentence>
    ...
    <sentence>sentence 10</sentence>
content:
<sentence>The risotto begins with a base of finely diced aromatic vegetables, typically onions, garlic, and sometimes celery or leeks.</sentence>
<sentence>These are gently sautéed in fat, usually butter or olive oil, until softened and translucent.</sentence>
<sentence>Arborio or Carnaroli rice, known for its high starch content, is then added and toasted briefly, coating each grain.</sentence>
<sentence>A splash of white wine is introduced, which is stirred until it evaporates, infusing the rice with its acidity.</sentence>
<sentence>The signature pattern emerges as hot broth is added ladle by ladle, with each addition being stirred until almost fully absorbed before the next is introduced.</sentence>
<sentence>This patient stirring process encourages the rice grains to release their starches, creating the creamy, luscious texture characteristic of risotto.</sentence>
<sentence>As the risotto cooks, the grains swell and become tender, but they retain a slight bite, known as al dente.</sentence>
<sentence>The constant movement prevents the rice from sticking to the bottom of the pan and ensures even cooking.</sentence>
<sentence>Towards the end of cooking, butter and grated hard cheese, most commonly Parmigiano-Reggiano, are vigorously stirred in, a technique known as mantecatura.</sentence>
<sentence>This final step emulsifies the fats and starches, resulting in a rich, glossy, and perfectly creamy risotto.</sentence>
